reason: Correct the active one-MI350 single-layer target interpretation after user clarification: MoE target is whole-layer routed expert GEMMs in one grouped/batched call, not per-expert latency.

active_single_mi350_targets.moe_target_scope: Whole MoE layer routed expert GEMM family, all experts together, forward plus backward, as one batched/grouped call family. Not per expert.

supersedes.reason: Used the old MoE expert GEMM M=12288 1.3 ms framing.
Updated in place to classify the M=12288 one-expert probe as reference-only and record the corrected 12.9 ms whole-MoE target.

moe_expert_gemm_family_estimate.caveat: This is a subblock reconstruction from segmented attribution, not a single fused grouped-MoE kernel timing. Use it as target interpretation evidence, not as a final pass/fail measurement.

full_routed_moe_blocks_for_context.read: These broader blocks include routing, activation, scale/reduce, score-grad, and attribution overhead. They should not be directly compared to the 12.9 ms expert-GEMM-only target.

decision.read: Under the corrected MoE target, the current Flash S4096 routed expert GEMM family estimate is close to the 12.9 ms anchor, while full-layer time and sparse MLA backward remain material gaps. MoE work should still pursue compact-native grouped training GEMM integration, but not because of a mistaken per-expert 1.3 ms target.
next_actions: Use 12.9 ms whole-MoE expert-GEMM fwd+bwd as the MoE comparison point in future summaries.
Keep direct one-expert M=12288 GEMM probes as library sanity checks only.
Once do-sonle-kernel is idle, run the new split-compressed sparse MLA backward primitive profile at S4096 to quantify the remaining attention backward path.
For MoE, measure whole-routed expert GEMM family timings directly when a fused/grouped kernel exists, rather than normalizing per-expert GEMM probes.
